# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Scope of Works — cascade-release downstream overrides on upstream edit

*Paste everything below the line into Claude Code. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-26.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow
it. Small fix-forward on PR #229.

## The bug

PR #229 made dimension overrides on the Scope of Works expanded item form
stick across save / reload — saved sqm/m³/tonnes values that differ from
the live auto-derive are flagged `dirty[k] = true` on load, so they display
as the saved override rather than reverting to the auto-derived value.

That fix works, but it introduced a second-order bug: **a downstream
override blocks live recompute when the user edits an upstream input.**

Owner-reported reproduction:
- On a scope item with saved m³ and tonnes values, change the m³ value.
- Tonnes does **not** recompute live.
- Only by deleting every dimension input and re-entering m³ and density
  does tonnes recompute.

## Root cause

In `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ScopeQuantitiesTable.tsx`, `setDim`
(roughly lines 577-582) only marks the **edited** field as dirty:

```ts
const setDim = (k: DimKey, v: string) => {
  setDims((s) => ({ ...s, [k]: v }));
  if (k === "sqm" || k === "m3" || k === "tonnes") {
    setDirty((d) => ({ ...d, [k]: true }));
  }
};
```

Combined with the post-#229 load logic that sets `dirty.tonnes = true`
when the saved tonnes differs from the auto-derive, this means:

- On load: `dirty.tonnes = true` (sticks the saved override).
- User edits m³: `setDim("m3", new)` → `dims.m3 = new`, `dirty.m3 = true`.
- `parsed.tonnes` evaluates to `Number(dims.tonnes)` because
  `dirty.tonnes && dims.tonnes !== ""` is still true (the line ~588-599
  memo).
- `computeDerivedDimensions(parsed)` returns `tonnes = parsed.tonnes`
  (the helper returns the explicit value when given non-null input).
- Tonnes never recomputes.

The "delete everything then re-edit" workaround works because clearing
tonnes makes `dims.tonnes === ""`, the `dirty.tonnes && dims.tonnes !== ""`
guard fails, `parsed.tonnes` becomes `null`, and the helper falls through
to the auto-derive.

## Fix

Extend `setDim` to **cascade-release downstream dirty flags** whenever the
user edits an upstream input. The dimension dependency graph:

| Edited field | Release dirty on … |
|---|---|
| length | sqm, m3, tonnes |
| height | sqm, m3, tonnes |
| depth | m3, tonnes |
| density | tonnes |
| sqm | m3, tonnes |
| m3 | tonnes |
| tonnes | (no releases — it's a leaf) |

Rewrite `setDim` along these lines (adapt to existing types):

```ts
const setDim = (k: DimKey, v: string) => {
  setDims((s) => ({ ...s, [k]: v }));
  setDirty((d) => {
    const next = { ...d };
    // Editing the field itself makes it a new override.
    if (k === "sqm" || k === "m3" || k === "tonnes") {
      next[k] = true;
    }
    // Editing an upstream releases all downstream overrides so the
    // live auto-derive can take over. The user can re-override the
    // downstream by typing into it again afterward.
    if (k === "length" || k === "height") {
      next.sqm = false;
      next.m3 = false;
      next.tonnes = false;
    } else if (k === "depth") {
      next.m3 = false;
      next.tonnes = false;
    } else if (k === "density") {
      next.tonnes = false;
    } else if (k === "sqm") {
      next.m3 = false;
      next.tonnes = false;
    } else if (k === "m3") {
      next.tonnes = false;
    }
    return next;
  });
};
```

## Expected behavior after fix

- Saved overrides still display correctly on load (unchanged from #229).
- Editing an upstream input releases downstream dirty flags → the
  downstream field live-recomputes from the helper.
- Editing the field itself sets it as the new override (unchanged).
- After an upstream edit released a downstream, the user can re-override
  the downstream by typing into it.
- `persistDims` (line ~606) still does the right thing: it persists
  `dirty.X ? dims.X : derived.X`. After a release, `dirty.X` is false, so
  `derived.X` (the freshly recomputed auto value) gets saved.

## Phase 0 — pre-work

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `fix/scope-dim-cascade-release`.
  (If PR #229 hasn't merged yet, branch off `fix/scope-dim-override-display`
  instead and base the new PR on it; otherwise off `main` after #229
  merges.)
- Conflict-check `ScopeQuantitiesTable.tsx`.

## Tests

- Add unit tests covering each upstream → downstream release:
  - typing into length releases sqm/m3/tonnes overrides
  - typing into height releases sqm/m3/tonnes overrides
  - typing into depth releases m3/tonnes overrides
  - typing into density releases tonnes override
  - typing into sqm releases m3/tonnes overrides
  - typing into m3 releases tonnes override
  - typing into tonnes does not release anything
- The existing #229 override-on-load tests should still pass (a freshly
  loaded saved override remains dirty until the user touches an upstream).
- Run `pnpm test:web:logic` and the §6 pre-PR checklist.

## PR — do NOT auto-merge

- Title: `[5A] Scope of Works — cascade-release downstream overrides on upstream edit`
- Body: the bug, the dependency-graph fix, files modified; §6 checklist
  green; no new env vars / migrations.
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- Create the PR and stop. Do not enable auto-merge. Owner (or Cowork)
  will live-test on a scope item: load with saved tonnes override, edit
  m³, confirm tonnes recomputes; then re-override tonnes manually,
  confirm it sticks.

## Scope

Only the cascade-release behavior in `setDim`. Do not touch
`computeDerivedDimensions`, `persistDims`, or any other part of the
component.
